proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_044` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months`, with a positive month-scale slope per 1-unit increase in `feature_044`.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_044` had an adjusted slope of +0.17568 `pfs_months` per 1-unit increase (SE 0.000512, z=343.32, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: Because `feature_044` dominated the first-pass screen, subsequent iterations tested whether other features retained independent associations after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_027 = 0`, after adjustment for the rest of the feature set.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_027 = 1` vs `feature_027 = 0` was associated with -1.18809 months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.01121, z=-106.00, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_027` appeared to have ordered adverse levels, so the next iteration tested level 2 separately.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027 = 2` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_027 = 0`, after adjustment for the rest of the feature set.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_027 = 2` vs `feature_027 = 0` was associated with -2.33376 months of `pfs_months` (SE 0.01575, z=-148.15, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: The level-2 decrement was roughly twice the level-1 decrement, motivating later interaction tests with `feature_044`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_039 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_039 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_039 = 1` was associated with -1.54890 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_039 = 0` (SE 0.01112, z=-139.33, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_039` was one of the largest adverse binary features and was carried forward into interaction testing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_046 = 1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_046 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_046 = 1` was associated with +1.10111 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_046 = 0` (SE 0.01069, z=103.03, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_046` was the strongest favorable binary feature and later showed marked subgroup heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_015 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_015 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_015 = 1` was associated with -0.98286 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_015 = 0` (SE 0.01684, z=-58.36, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_015` was adverse and less common, so later analyses tested whether it also modified the `feature_044` slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_037` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for the rest of the feature set.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_037` had an adjusted slope of -0.07685 months per 1-unit increase (SE 0.00132, z=-58.24, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: Continuous adverse predictors persisted after adjustment, not only binary subgroup indicators.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_031 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_031 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_031 = 1` was associated with -0.58187 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_031 = 0` (SE 0.01070, z=-54.40, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_031` had a moderate adverse main effect and was tested for interactions with favorable binary markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_004` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for the rest of the feature set.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_004` had an adjusted slope of +0.47517 months per 1-unit increase (SE 0.01015, z=46.79, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_004` was retained as a favorable continuous main-effect signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_062` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for the rest of the feature set.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_062` had an adjusted slope of -0.01889 months per 1-unit increase (SE 0.000419, z=-45.12, p<1e-300).

iterations.update: `feature_062` added another continuous adverse association independent of the dominant `feature_044` effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_069 = 1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_069 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_069 = 1` was associated with +0.53157 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_069 = 0` (SE 0.01492, z=35.63, p=5.02e-278).

iterations.update: `feature_069` was favorable overall and became a candidate modifier of the `feature_046` association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_063 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_063 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_063 = 1` was associated with -0.47073 months of `pfs_months` relative to `feature_063 = 0` (SE 0.01418, z=-33.20, p=1.16e-241).

iterations.update: `feature_063` was adverse overall and was later tested as an antagonist of favorable subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_010 = 1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_010 = 0` in an unadjusted subgroup comparison.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted two-group comparison: mean `pfs_months` was 4.82351 for `feature_010 = 1` vs 4.46156 for `feature_010 = 0`, a +0.36196 month difference (z=16.22, p=3.86e-59).

iterations.update: `feature_010` looked favorable in marginal comparisons, so later binary-pair analyses considered whether that advantage depended on other binary features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_025 = 1` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_025 = 0` in an unadjusted subgroup comparison.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted two-group comparison: mean `pfs_months` was 4.76485 for `feature_025 = 1` vs 4.65045 for `feature_025 = 0`, a +0.11440 month difference (z=4.88, p=1.04e-6).

iterations.update: This marginal effect was much smaller than the dominant adjusted signals, so it was treated as secondary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `feature_033` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for the rest of the feature set.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_033` had an adjusted slope of -0.000519 months per 1-unit increase (SE 0.0000630, z=-8.23, p=1.93e-16).

iterations.update: `feature_033` was statistically robust but small on the month scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_040 = 1` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `feature_040 = 0`, after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: All-feature OLS model: `feature_040 = 1` had a small positive adjusted coefficient of +0.01287 months, not the hypothesized adverse direction, and was not significant (SE 0.01024, z=1.26, p=0.209).

iterations.update: `feature_040` did not show a reliable main-effect association, although it appeared in exploratory interaction screens.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `feature_044` and `pfs_months` is convex upward: adding a centered `feature_044` squared term has a positive coefficient after the linear term is included.

analyses.result_summary: OLS model with `feature_044` and centered `feature_044` squared: the quadratic term was +0.000604 months per squared centered unit (SE 0.0000585, z=10.32, p=5.94e-25).

iterations.update: The dominant `feature_044` association was mostly linear but had a statistically detectable upward curvature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive slope of `feature_044` on `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_039 = 1` than among patients with `feature_039 = 0`, after adjustment for major main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: `feature_044 * feature_039` was -0.01076 months per `feature_044` unit (SE 0.00136, z=-7.91, p=2.55e-15). Stratified unadjusted slopes were +0.17952 for `feature_039 = 0` and +0.16837 for `feature_039 = 1`.

iterations.update: `feature_039` both lowered baseline `pfs_months` and attenuated the favorable `feature_044` gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive slope of `feature_044` on `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_015 = 1` than among patients with `feature_015 = 0`, after adjustment for major main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: `feature_044 * feature_015` was -0.00675 months per `feature_044` unit (SE 0.00206, z=-3.27, p=0.00107). Stratified unadjusted slopes were +0.17645 for `feature_015 = 0` and +0.16884 for `feature_015 = 1`.

iterations.update: `feature_015` was another adverse subgroup in which the `feature_044` association was modestly attenuated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive slope of `feature_044` on `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_027 = 2` than among patients with `feature_027 = 0`.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction model with `feature_044`, `feature_027` indicators, and their interactions: `feature_044 * (feature_027 = 2)` was -0.01291 months per `feature_044` unit (SE 0.00219, z=-5.88, p=4.02e-9).

iterations.update: The most adverse `feature_027` level also reduced the marginal benefit associated with higher `feature_044`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive slope of `feature_044` on `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_027 = 1` than among patients with `feature_027 = 0`.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction model with `feature_044`, `feature_027` indicators, and their interactions: `feature_044 * (feature_027 = 1)` was -0.00291 months per `feature_044` unit and did not reach significance (SE 0.00158, z=-1.84, p=0.0654).

iterations.update: Only `feature_027 = 2`, not `feature_027 = 1`, showed strong evidence of modifying the `feature_044` slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive slope of `feature_044` on `pfs_months` is weaker among patients with `feature_046 = 1` than among patients with `feature_046 = 0`, after adjustment for major main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: `feature_044 * feature_046` was -0.00252 months per `feature_044` unit and was borderline but not significant at 0.05 (SE 0.00131, z=-1.93, p=0.0542).

iterations.update: `feature_046` had a strong favorable main effect, but little evidence that it modified the continuous `feature_044` slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_046 = 1` and `feature_031 = 1` is associated with lower `pfs_months` than expected from adding their separate subgroup mean effects.

analyses.result_summary: Four-cell difference-in-differences: the `feature_046 * feature_031` interaction was -1.62301 months (SE 0.04724, z=-34.36, p=1.11e-258). Cell means were 4.307 (`00`), 5.966 (`10`), 4.316 (`01`), and 4.352 (`11`).

iterations.update: `feature_031` nearly eliminated the favorable marginal pattern seen with `feature_046`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_046 = 1` and `feature_069 = 1` is associated with higher `pfs_months` than expected from adding their separate subgroup mean effects.

analyses.result_summary: Four-cell difference-in-differences: the `feature_046 * feature_069` interaction was +1.59440 months (SE 0.04784, z=33.32, p=1.69e-243). Cell means were 4.303 (`00`), 4.285 (`10`), 4.313 (`01`), and 5.890 (`11`).

iterations.update: `feature_069` identified a subgroup in which the favorable association of `feature_046` was concentrated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The joint presence of `feature_046 = 1` and `feature_063 = 1` is associated with lower `pfs_months` than expected from adding their separate subgroup mean effects.

analyses.result_summary: Four-cell difference-in-differences: the `feature_046 * feature_063` interaction was -1.36373 months (SE 0.05511, z=-24.75, p=3.31e-135). Cell means were 4.307 (`00`), 5.657 (`10`), 4.322 (`01`), and 4.308 (`11`).

iterations.update: `feature_063` also identified a subgroup in which the favorable marginal association of `feature_046` disappeared.
